title: runtime SDK run-local-pipeline goal_autopilot
purpose: I2.1 defines runtime sdk run local pipeline goal autopilot for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools.
scope: Owns `I2.1` only; siblings under `I2` must not duplicate this spec.
Aligns with minimal HITL: H1 plan, H2 blocker, H3 sign-off ([INTRO-1.2](INTRO-1.2-human-touchpoint-contract-h1-h2-h3.md)).
Conflicts resolve in favor of [Vision §11 — Branch I — Runtime & integration plane](../../full-automation-vision-and-hierarchy.md#11-branch-i-runtime-integration-plane).
behavior_steps: When `goal_autopilot` is active, `scripts/automation/run-local-pipeline.py` wakes on schedule and runs the continue skill without an operator typing `/continue`, reading `next_action` from `journal/state.json` under the same contract as IDE pursuit ([A3.4](A3.4-sdk-daemon-run-local-pipeline-24-7.md)).
Before each daemon iteration, S0 `check-pipeline-blocked` runs; gates, blocking questions, budget caps, or corrupt state stop the loop and surface H2 instead of advancing pursuit.

Each iteration spawns Cursor agents or SDK equivalents per `docs/operator/model-policy.json`—genius-tier conductor for orchestration, economy workers for bounded tasks—while dual-writing journal and state after every step.
When in-scope work is complete, the daemon routes to [goal_verify](A1.3-goal-verify-command-meta-test.md) rather than another implement task, then holds for H3 sign-off if verification passes.
Worker run metadata feeds [H5](H5-worker-runs.md) audit; on integrity validation failure or repeated verify failure, autopilot halts with structured H2 and `last_failure` recorded in the journal.
edge_cases: Operator closes laptop mid-loop â€” state.json must resume from last good dual-write.
Concurrent manual edit to queue JSON â€” conductor reloads queue each wake; last writer wins with journal note.
Edge case `I2.1` variant 3: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.
Edge case `I2.1` variant 4: verify state dual-write before continuing pursuit.

**False complete:** Item marked done without artifact â†’ audit-hierarchy-depth.py re-enqueues deepen pass.
**Scope bleed:** Worker edits journal/state during planning-only expansion â†’ forbidden in vision-expansion-prompt.
**Stale design:** Upstream vision Â§ changes â†’ reconcile-stale adds deepen items for affected ids.

Add unit test under tests/unit/test_i2_1.py when script exists.

narrative: I2.1 defines runtime sdk run local pipeline goal autopilot for the agent-driven expert system. Runtime & integration — IDE, SDK, headless, MCP tools. In practice, the system when `goal_autopilot` is active, `scripts/automation/run-local-pipeline.py` wakes on schedule and runs the continue skill without an operator typing `/continue`, reading `next_action` from `journal/state.json` under the same contract as ide pursuit ([a3.4](a3.4-sdk-daemon-run-local-pipeline-24-7.md)), then before each daemon iteration, s0 `check-pipeline-blocked` runs; gates, blocking questions, budget caps, or corrupt state stop the loop and surface h2 instead of advancing pursuit.
reader_narrative:
<!-- prose-source: agent plane-i 2026-06-28 -->

`scripts/automation/run-local-pipeline.py` is the SDK/local daemon entry for goal_autopilot—repeated continue steps without manual /continue, using the same journal/state contract as IDE pursuit ([A3.4](A3.4-sdk-daemon-run-local-pipeline-24-7.md)).

The daemon runs S0 check-pipeline-blocked before each iteration and stops on gates, budget exhaustion, or integrity failures. It spawns Cursor agents or SDK equivalents per model-policy; unattended 24/7 operation still respects H2/H3 stops. Pair with worker-runs audit ([H5](H5-worker-runs.md)).
